# 3D POLE Modeler

> Interactive 3D structural viewer for the human DNA Polymerase epsilon (POLE) catalytic subunit (p261, 2,286 AA, UniProt Q07864). Browser-based, built with Three.js and Mol*, combining a procedural schematic viewer, real-coordinate PDB viewer, variant analysis, and frameshift mutation visualization. Apache 2.0 licensed.

The project provides four interconnected viewers for exploring POLE domain architecture, catalytic mechanisms, and cancer-associated mutations:

- **Schematic Viewer** (`index.html`): Procedural cartoon-ribbon model with 8 functional domains, two-metal-ion active sites, DNA duplex, PCNA sliding clamp, [4Fe-4S] cluster, Zn-finger motifs, and 9 mutation hotspot markers. Interactive controls for toggles, color schemes (Default/PyMOL/Accessible), keyboard shortcuts, and distance measurement.
- **Mol* Coordinate Viewer** (`molstar.html`): Real atomic coordinates from PDB 9F6D/9F6E/9F6F (Roske & Yeeles 2024) and 9B8S (He et al. 2024). Supports cartoon/ball-and-stick/spacefill/surface representations with domain, B-factor, chain-id, and secondary-structure coloring.
- **Variant Analysis** (`variant.html`): ACMG classification with auditable Bayesian evidence codes, mutational signature attribution (SBS10a/b/28/14), computational predictors (AlphaMissense, REVEL, CADD), and DDG stability estimates for 9 characterized pathogenic variants.
- **Mutation Viewer** (`mutation.html`): Side-by-side wild-type vs. truncated visualization of the c.138del germline frameshift (p.Leu46Phefs*8) with reading frame analysis and domain retention chart.

Key technologies: Three.js (WebGL), Mol* (molecular viewer), ES modules, Vitest, ESLint, Julia pipeline for data processing.

## Docs

- [README](README.md): Project overview, features, viewer descriptions, keyboard shortcuts, development setup, limitations, and scientific references
- [Integration Guide](INTEGRATION.md): Data layer contract between canonical JSON and JS consumers; schema, loader API, and migration notes
- [Contributing](CONTRIBUTING.md): How to contribute, coding standards, PR process
- [Security Policy](SECURITY.md): Vulnerability reporting

## Core JS Modules

- [pole-viewer.js](js/pole-viewer.js): Main 3D schematic viewer engine (~1,750 lines); POLEViewer class with ribbon rendering, domain architecture, active sites, DNA, PCNA, mutation markers, controls, and render loop
- [molstar-viewer.js](js/molstar-viewer.js): Mol* wrapper class; PDB loading, MVS-based domain coloring, representation switching, label projection, and zoom control
- [mutation-viewer.js](js/mutation-viewer.js): Frameshift mutation viewer; side-by-side wild-type/mutant rendering, domain retention chart, reading frame visualization
- [ribbon.js](js/ribbon.js): Geometry primitives for cartoon ribbon (helix tubes, strand arrows, loop tubes, secondary-structure segmentation)
- [pole-geometry.js](js/pole-geometry.js): Pure utility functions for procedural backbone generation (PRNG, domain backbone, inter-domain linkers)
- [pole-palette.js](js/pole-palette.js): Shared THREE.Color palette and color scheme definitions (Default, PyMOL, Accessible)

## Data Layer

- [domainConfig.js](js/data/domainConfig.js): Shared domain definitions (DOMAINS, DOMAIN_ORDER, MUTATIONS, DOMAIN_COLORS) consumed by all viewers
- [poleData.js](js/data/poleData.js): Async loader and accessor for canonical JSON; schema validation, reference resolution, provenance footer
- [Canonical Data JSON](data/pole_canonical_data.json): Single source of truth for domain boundaries, active site residues, metal sites, ClinVar-anchored variants, signatures, and references
- [Data Schema](data/pole_canonical_data.schema.json): JSON Schema for validating canonical data

## Pages

- [Schematic Viewer](index.html): Main procedural ribbon viewer
- [Mol* Coordinate Viewer](molstar.html): PDB coordinate viewer
- [Variant Analysis](variant.html): ACMG classification and signature analysis
- [Mutation Viewer](mutation.html): c.138del frameshift visualization
- [AlphaFold Viewer](alphafold.html): AlphaFold predicted structure

## Pipeline

- [Pipeline README](pipeline/README.md): Julia data processing pipeline documentation
- [Pipeline Entry Point](pipeline/run.jl): Orchestrates PDB parsing, DSSP, conservation, variant scoring, ACMG classification, and JSON output
